findings.evidence: `GradingInteger.lean:55` proves `JNum_self` and `:61` proves `J_self`; `JFunction.lean:154-168` is where `JNum`, `JNum_def`, `J`, and `J_def` are defined and its module doc says it is the point-set-level `J` API.
findings.fix: Move these two theorems to `TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/JFunction.lean` near `JNum_def`/`J_def`, then have `GradingInteger.lean` use them from that earlier file.
findings.issue: `GridPoint.JNum_self` and `GridPoint.J_self` are generic point-set facts about `JNum`/`J`, not grading-integrality facts, so putting them in `GradingInteger.lean` hides broadly useful `JFunction` API behind a later grading module.

summary: The grading integrality declarations are in the right grid area and the module import is not broadly wrong, but two generic point-set `J` lemmas are placed in the later grading-integrality file instead of the `JFunction` API they elaborate.
